system.damage: tree-rending claws d10+7 w/ disadvantage (reach, close, forceful, messy, 1 piercing)
system.specialQuality: poor vision and hearing  excellent sense of smell
system.moves:
- Not really notice something
- Tear up trees like a child tears up grass
- Swat at something that annoys it
- Flail about in a rage (+area), then get tired

Think of something like a badger or a sloth, but big as a house: 20 feet long, 9 feet at the shoulder, forearms nearly 12 feet long when you include those shovel-like claws. Mostly they just lumber about the Steplands, looking for their favorite trees to tear up and chew through, sometimes clearing whole slopes. Just about every- thing gives them a wide berth, when they can. Even apex predators like a kleztigr don’t think they’re worth the trouble. You’d think that they’d be easy to spot and avoid, and mostly you’d be right. But some- times you’ll round a bend or clear a slope and there one’ll be, just plodding along or munching on a log or taking a nap, and you’d best try to circle around, quiet-like.
